_note: SYNTHETIC — invented district and facts. Exercises bumping/competency-to-teach-the-whole-position + tie-breaking lottery sequencing.
basis: PKS reduction of particular kinds of services (eliminating selected elective/vocational courses) plus an ADA decline.
reductions: elimination of the plastics / industrial-technology course

proposed_skips.claimed_special_skill: PPS credential plus bilingual capacity for an EL-counseling caseload

bumping_disputes.senior_employee_role: industrial-arts teacher whose plastics course is eliminated
bumping_disputes.claims: competent to teach all of general industrial arts and ROP currently assigned to a junior teacher
bumping_disputes.district_position: senior teacher is not competent to teach the full range of the junior's general industrial-arts / ROP assignment

bumping_disputes.claims: credentialed and competent to bump a junior teacher retained for driver training
bumping_disputes.district_position: no showing the junior is retained for driver training specifically; speculative

tiebreak.criteria: first date of paid service; then credentials held, additional authorizations, and semester units beyond the BA; final tie resolved by lottery
tiebreak.lottery_detail: Two teachers shared the same first date of paid service; after applying the credential/authorization/units criteria they remained tied, so the district broke the final tie by lottery.

board_resolution_criteria: Tie-break criteria adopted in the PKS resolution; the resolution authorizes a lottery only after the objective criteria are exhausted.
attorney_concerns: Can the senior plastics teacher bump the junior into the general industrial-arts/ROP position by claiming general competence? And we used a lottery to break a final tie after applying our other criteria — is that defensible, or should we have used some further criterion first?
